The '''''Warrior''-class gunship''' was a deadly addition to the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] fleet produced under the [[New Class Modernization Program]].

==Characteristics==
These small but powerful warships were designed for one purpose, which was to smash enemy formations by destroying their main combat ships. They were armed with 10 [[Turbolaser/Legends|turbolaser]] cannons, 8 laser cannons (with at least some in twin [[Barrel/Legends|barrel]] batteries), and 4 [[Concussion missile/Legends|concussion missile]] tubes, plus a number of antimissile weapons.<ref name="CTD" /> ''Warrior''-class gunships packed enough firepower to threaten heavy cruisers, and their racks of [[Concussion missile/Legends|CM-9 concussion missiles]], the same weapon carried by the [[Majestic-class heavy cruiser|''Majestic''-class heavy cruiser]],<ref name="TT" /> could even cripple [[Star Destroyer/Legends|Star Destroyers]].<ref name="CTD" />

Following New Class practice, the ''Warrior''-class gunship was built on the same hull as the [[Agave-class picket ship|''Agave''-class picket ship]]. Like the ''Agave'', the ''Warrior'' was 190 meters long and was equipped with a Class 1 [[Hyperdrive/Legends|hyperdrive]]. At sublight speeds, it was driven by a bank of at least six engines. These ships had a small crew of only 44 people, along with 36 gunners and room for 10 additional passengers.<ref name="CTD" />

In spite of their strengths, the ''Warrior''-class were designed for fast attacks, and not for extended, close-range combat against [[Cruiser/Legends|cruisers]].<ref name="CTD" /> While they were significantly more robust than their ''Agave''-class cousins, their [[Deflector shield/Legends|deflector shields]] would be overwhelmed in a matter of minutes by heavy cruiser [[Turbolaser/Legends|turbolasers]], and their [[Particle shield/Legends|particle shields]] could be brought down by a single missile salvo. This vulnerability was seen in the [[Battle of ILC-905]], when the New Republic gunship [[Vanguard (Warrior-class)|''Vanguard'']] engaged two [[Yevetha]]n [[Aramadia-class thrustship|''Aramadia''-class thrustships]]. Although ''Vanguard'' played a major role in destroying one of the enemy cruisers, it was obliterated after a few volleys of enemy missiles.<ref name="TT" />

==History==
To perform their mission, these ships normally moved ahead of the main fleet to make first contact with the enemy. ''Warrior''-class gunships had enough laser cannons to fend off starfighters, while their armor provided protection beyond the typical starfighter's ability to do damage.<ref name="CTD" />

Once a ''Warrior'' group had penetrated enemy screening lines, they targeted and destroyed the enemy's main ships before withdrawing into [[Hyperspace/Legends|hyperspace]]. Individual gunships could also be used in smaller squadrons, paired with cruisers as robust and well-armed support ships, capable of scouting more aggressively than sensor pickets or starfighters.<ref name="CTD" /> ''Warrior'' gunships were also used to clear corridors for surface forces to land after planetary guns had been disabled.

==Behind the scenes==
The only official image of the ''Warrior''-class gunship is found in the RPG sourcebook ''Cracken's Threat Dossier''. Like the pictures of other New Class ships, this picture contradicts information from the [[Black Fleet Crisis]] novels, on which ''Cracken's Threat Dossier'' is based. The ''Warrior'' and ''Agave'' classes look nothing alike, even though they are supposed to be built on the same hull.
